title: 2020 FOMC Press Releases
summary: This Federal Reserve page is an index listing all 13 FOMC statements and projection releases from 2020. The page does not contain the substantive text of individual statements; it only provides dates and titles with links. Individual FOMC statement pages must be crawled separately for archiveable content.
key_points: The page indexes 13 FOMC events from 2020: 11 statements, 3 projection releases (June, September, December)
Emergency FOMC statements were issued on 3/3/2020, 3/15/2020, and 3/23/2020 during the initial COVID-19 crisis
Key rate action period was March 2020 with two emergency cuts preceding the scheduled March 17-18 meeting
Regular scheduled statements issued approximately every 6 weeks per FOMC calendar
Last Update on the page is February 26, 2026 indicating the index is maintained but content is historical
Individual statement content not present in this source—full text requires navigating to separate press release pages
why_it_matters: This listing page is not itself archiveable. Researchers must access individual FOMC statement pages to retrieve the actual policy content (rate decisions, forward guidance, balance sheet details). The 2020 FOMC statements contain critical macro catalyst information including emergency rate cuts and QE programs.
macro_context: 2020 FOMC decisions were the primary macro catalysts for global markets that year. The Fed's emergency response to COVID-19—rate cuts, QE expansion, repo operations, and forward guidance changes—directly shaped market structure and risk-asset behavior throughout the year. The actual statements from March 2020 are particularly significant for understanding the Fed's crisis response framework.
market_structure_context: This source does not contain market structure information. The 2020 FOMC statements did reference changes to Fed operations, balance sheet expansion, and the introduction of new lending facilities affecting repo markets and money markets, but that content is not present here.

issues_found: Source is a listing_page per source metadata; Rule 5 mandates rejection for listing_page type
Source contains only dates and titles of FOMC releases, no substantive statement text
event_type set to 'fomc_statement' is misleading—source contains no FOMC statement content
Published_at (2020-01-29) incorrectly reflects earliest listed date rather than source publication date
No specific policy decisions, vote counts, or forward guidance can be extracted from this index
No archival value as substantive content—only navigation metadata

notes: Non-archiveable listing page. The Federal Reserve maintains this as an index to their 2020 FOMC statements. Each listed item (e.g., '12/16/2020 Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement') links to a separate page with the actual statement text. For a proper research archive, individual FOMC statement pages should be crawled separately. Historical record (2020) with TODAY being 2026-03-31—over 5 years old, but 2020 FOMC decisions remain historically significant for understanding Fed crisis response framework.
